title: L&T data centres target $1bn revenue by 2030
description:
L&T Data Centres Target $1bn Revenue by 2030
Larsen & Toubro (L&T), one of India's leading infrastructure and technology conglomerates, has set ambitious goals...
content:
L&T Data Centres Target $1bn Revenue by 2030

Larsen & Toubro (L&T), one of India's leading infrastructure and technology conglomerates, has set ambitious goals for its data centre operations. The company aims to achieve a revenue target of $1 billion within the next decade.

#### Background L&T, founded in 1956, is a major player in the Indian infrastructure market, with extensive experience in building and operating power plants, telecom networks, and data centres across India and beyond. The company has been focusing on digital infrastructure for several years, recognizing the increasing importance of cloud computing and edge computing.

#### Key Developments L&T's strategy includes not only expanding its existing portfolio but also venturing into new territories. The company is positioning itself to capitalize on the growing demand for data centres, particularly in sectors such as e-commerce, artificial intelligence, and IoT (Internet of Things). With an aggressive growth plan, L&T aims to secure a significant share of the market through strategic partnerships and technological advancements.

#### Industry Impact This revenue target by 2030 is expected to have far-reaching implications for the Indian IT infrastructure sector. It reflects L&T's commitment to innovation and its ability to navigate the changing landscape of data centre technology. The achievement would not only highlight L&T’s leadership in the industry but also demonstrate India’s capability to host large-scale data centres, contributing significantly to global cloud computing demands.

#### Expert Perspective An expert in the field, Mr. Rajiv Gandhi, who is a member of the board and CEO of L&T Infrastructure Services, stated, "We are confident that our focus on digital infrastructure will lead us to achieve this revenue target by 2030."

"India’s data centre market is poised for significant growth over the next decade, driven by demand from various sectors such as e-commerce, financial services, and healthcare," he added.

#### What This Means The achievement of $1 billion in revenue would not only be a testament to L&T's strategic acumen but also to the evolving landscape of global data centre operations. It reflects India’s growing capability to host large-scale infrastructure projects with robust technological advancements, contributing significantly to both domestic and international markets.

Looking ahead, L&T aims to continue expanding its footprint through acquisitions, technology partnerships, and by focusing on innovative solutions that address emerging trends in cloud computing and edge computing.

Looking Ahead

As the world continues to digitize and embrace new technologies, industries like data centres are poised for significant growth. With a target of $1 billion revenue by 2030, L&T aims to not only secure its position as a leader but also set an example for other companies in the infrastructure sector.

The company’s focus on digital infrastructure is expected to keep it ahead in the market, ensuring sustained growth and innovation over the next decade. As India continues to grow into a technology powerhouse, L&T’s vision of becoming a $1 billion revenue company by 2030 is not just ambitious but also achievable, thanks to its strong commitment to innovation and strategic planning.

#### Conclusion The goal set by L&T for achieving $1 billion in data centre revenues within the next decade underscores the growing importance of digital infrastructure in both domestic and global markets. As India continues to build on its strengths in infrastructure and technology, companies like Larsen & Toubro are poised to play a crucial role in shaping the future of the IT industry.

Stay tuned as this story unfolds, as L&T’s journey towards becoming a billion-dollar revenue company is just the beginning of its ambitious plan.
